148 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 1:

James are the upstream folks requiring that we use "if (!ptr)" instead of "if (ptr == NULL)"? I can't find any mention of this in Documentation/CodingStyle.

Patch Set 1:

Yes 'if (!ptr)' is required over 'if (ptr == NULL)'. The upstream checkpatch reported if (ptr == NULL) or if (ptr != NULL) as style errors. Also required is if (rc) or if (!rc) form as well. This is not reported by checkpatch yet but Greg has demand this change as well.

Patch Set 1:

Andreas this initial patch cut was done just to port the upstream socklnd as it is to master stack. Yes many cleanups need to be done. I plan on break up this patch soon.

Well if we want to sync the Intel tree to upstream this work needs to be done. If you are against this I fine but the time is coming soon when we have to arrange working with the LNet layer from the upstream instead of continue to work against the Intel/OpenSFS branch. In other words in one way or another the LNet code in master is going away soon.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

From the feedback to appears people feel that its better to keep upstream a separate tree from master so this work will be dropped.
Restored

commit.subject: LU-6142 socklnd: change UAPI typedefs to proper structure
commit.message:
LU-6142 socklnd: change UAPI typedefs to proper structure

The upstream kernel requires proper structures so
convert all the UAPI typedefs in socklnd.h.

Remove the inline function socklnd_init_msg.
Its only used by the kernel code and functions
are not allowed in UAPI headers.

Change-Id: I4120c5abc4dda456289146452ed61e0162062cea
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/18506
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
